name: Asparagus (Generic)
recipeInstructions:
Asparagus is low-calorie and spiked with vitamins A & C. Allow about
To Prepare:
Break off tough ends as far down as the stalks snap easily. For spears, tie them with string. Or, cut each stalk into 1-inch pieces.
Cooking Spears:
In a deep, narrow pan or asparagus pot, bring salted water to boiling.
Place asparagus upright in the pot. Bring to boiling point, then reduce heat. Boil uncovered for 5 minutes. Cover and boil an additional 2-3 minutes longer. Drain.
Cooking Pieces:
Boil lower stalk pieces in salted water that is shallow enough (1 inch) for 6 minutes. Add tips, cover, and cook until tender, about 1-2 minutes. Drain.
Steaming:
Place a steamer or basket in water that is shallow enough (1/2 inch) so it does not touch the basket. Place asparagus in the basket. Cover tightly and bring to boiling. Reduce heat and steam until tender, about 6 minutes.
Microwave:
Cover and microwave spears or pieces of asparagus with 1/4 cup water in a 2-quart casserole on high for 4 minutes. Turn asparagus over. Continue microwaving until tender-crisp, about 3 more minutes. Let stand 1 minute and drain.
